zh.lifeSummary: 當Sophia von Berg於1099年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 伯格時，她的父親Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen30歲，母親Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg26歲。她和Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1144年7月27日享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sophia von Berg于1099年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 伯格时，她的父亲Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen30岁，母亲Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg26岁。她和Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1144年7月27日享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Sophia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek word meaning ‘wisdom’. The Eastern cult of  St Sophia  arose as a result of misinterpretation of the phrase Hagia Sophia ‘holy wisdom’ as if it meant ‘St Sophia’. The name became popular in England in the 17th and 18th centuries—the heroine of  Fielding's  novel Tom Jones (  1749 ) is called  Sophia Weston—and  has been increasing in popularity since the 1990s. In Scotland it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Beathag .

variants.name: Sofia
meanings: variant spelling of   Sophia .

en.lifeSummary: When Sophia von Berg was born in 1099, in Berg, Holy Roman Empire, her father, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, was 30 and her mother, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, was 26. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. She died on 27 July 1144, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sophia von Berg est née en 1099, à Herzogtum Berg, Saint-Empire romain, son père, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. Elle est décédée le 27 juillet 1144, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sophia von Berg 1099 in Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen 30 und ihre Mutter Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg 26. Sie hatte mit Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 27. Juli 1144 im Alter von 45 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sophia von Berg è nata il nel 1099 nel Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, sua padre, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, aveva 26 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. Morì il 27 luglio 1144, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sophia von Bergが1099年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチハーゾグトゥム バーグで生まれた時，父Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingenは30歳，母Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburgは26歳であった。彼女はOta II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomouckýとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1144年7月27日に45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sophia von Berg(이)가 1099년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 허초그투 버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1144년 7월 27일 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sophia von Berg nasceu em 1099, em Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, tinha 26 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. Ela faleceu em 27 de julho de 1144, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sophia von Berg родилась в 1099 г. в местности хирзогтум бирг, хилигесь рoмищесь раич, её отцу по имени Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, 26. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. Она умерла 27 июля 1144 г. в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sophia von Berg nació en 1099, en Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Ota II. Černý Přemyslovec Olomoucký. Murió el 27 de julio de 1144, a la edad de 45 años.
